description:
## Goal
Lock destructuring's parity gates across all four legs (interp, run, build, repl).

## Depends on
- E3 (so irrefutable destructures don't trip warnings-as-errors).
- E5 (so the interp leg passes for list primitives).

## Fixtures to add
- Irrefutable tuple destructure: `{a, b} = pair()`.
- Refutable list-cons destructure: `[h | t] = xs` on success path (mismatch crash is not parity-tested via matrix — see note).
- Mixed nested: `{[h | t], y} = e`.

Each runs through all four legs and asserts identical stdout.

## Note on runtime-crash testing
The fixture matrix only asserts exit 0 (`RunOutcome::Ok`). It has no infrastructure for "this fixture should crash with `:match_error`." Refutable destructure's crash semantics are verified informally during E5's interp probes, not via the matrix. A separate ticket can add matrix support for runtime-crash fixtures if needed (out of scope here).

## Done when
- New fixtures live under `fixtures/` with `input.fz` + matrix-required snapshots.
- All four legs pass for each fixture.
title: E6: fixture-matrix parity for destructuring (all four legs)
